notes: Frozen contract for the six v3-remaining CLI surface families whose parity with the Python `agentera` stable CLI has not yet been pinned. The fixture is the contract reference for `packages/cli/test/cli/npmParityMatrix.test.ts` — every row in the parity matrix reads its expected shape and forbidden-shape sentinel from here. The `python_commit` field pins the Python CLI source (commit on the agentera `main` branch) the structural contract is locked to. The `normalizeEnvelope` block is the canonical normalization spec applied to live envelopes before drift classification (timestamp → ISO date, hash → 0x + 8 hex, path → forward-slash). Per-family `python_commit` mirrors the top-level pin so the matrix can be exercised per-family when the families land in T2-T7. The `expectedShape` block is structural (required keys + value types + literal pins); the `forbiddenSubstrings` block is the per-family sentinel — a substring the TS side MUST NOT emit because the Python side does not. T1 marks every row in the matrix as `intentional_version_break: true` (the six surfaces are not yet ported in this commit); T2-T7 close the rows by removing the `version_break` flag and asserting `drift_direction === 'equal'`.

normalizeEnvelope.description: Canonical normalization applied to live TS envelopes before they are compared to the Python oracle pin. The helper lives in `packages/cli/test/cli/npmParityMatrix.test.ts` and reads these rules verbatim; do not duplicate the rules in test code.

timestamp.description: Any value matching ISO 8601 with a time component (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS[.fff][Z|+HH:MM]`) is reduced to the date (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Date-only values pass through unchanged. Non-string values pass through unchanged.

hash.description: Any value matching a hex hash of 8+ characters (with or without an algorithm prefix like `sha256:`) is reduced to `0x` + the first 8 hex characters. Algorithm-prefixed hashes are stripped of the prefix first. Values shorter than 8 hex characters pass through unchanged. Non-string values pass through unchanged.

path.description: Any string value that is a filesystem path is normalized to forward-slash separators. Backslashes are replaced with forward slashes. Windows drive-letter prefixes (`C:\`) are lowercased. UNC paths (`\\server\share`) are normalized to `//server/share`. The normalization is applied recursively to nested object keys whose names are in a `pathKeys` allowlist (path, file, dir, root, home, source_root, app_home, user_data_root, managed_app_root, persisted_artifact_path, agentera_home).

driftDirectionTaxonomy.description: The four-valued drift classification applied to every parity-matrix row.

values.equal: Normalized TS envelope matches the Python oracle pin; the row is in lockstep and the test passes.
values.ts_smaller: Normalized TS envelope is missing keys/fields the Python oracle emits; the TS side is smaller than the Python side. The test fails unless `version_break: true` is set on the row.
values.python_smaller: Normalized TS envelope has extra keys/fields the Python oracle does not emit; the Python side is smaller than the TS side. The test fails unless `version_break: true` is set on the row.
values.intentional_version_break: The row has `version_break: true` set, classifying any drift (ts_smaller or python_smaller) as intentional; the test passes. T2-T7 will close these rows by removing the flag and re-classifying as `equal`.

artifact_validation.description: The seven `check validate <family> --format json` subcommands (capability, capability-contract, cross-capability, lifecycle-adapters, app-home-contract, vocabularyAuthority, selfAudit). T2 ports the 7 surfaces; T1 pins the structural contract and marks the row as `intentional_version_break`.

compaction.description: `check compact` apply + dry-run + retention caps (10/40/50 from hooks/common). T3 closes this row; dry-run gate and apply paths match the Python oracle pin.
forbiddenSubstrings: Cannot create a string longer than 0x1fffffe8
yaml.YAMLError: mapping values are not allowed

verify_eval.description: `check verify eval` family (eval semantic + eval skills, with --dry-run and --run paths; smoke family is retired on npm). T5 ports the safety wrappers and parity pins; gap `gap-verify-eval-family` closes when drift_direction === 'equal'.
forbiddenSubstrings: verify smoke is retired on the npm self-contained CLI
live runtime call attempted without explicit --run

doctor_upgrade_safety.description: `doctor` and `upgrade --dry-run` with the canonical Decision 54 lifecycle vocabulary (up_to_date, outdated, repair_needed, manual_review_needed, ready_to_apply, applied, no_changes_needed); smoke checks; next-major doctor for the npm channel. T4 ports the safety rails; T1 pins the structural contract and marks the row as `intentional_version_break`.

usage_stats_consent.description: `stats` + `stats refresh --dry-run` + `stats refresh --consent local-history`; explicit consent enforcement; size-bounded corpus reads; no top-level `agentera corpus` (per resolved-ledger TODOs L84, L85). T6 closes this row; plain `stats` missing-corpus JSON matches the Python oracle pin.

runtime_adapter_hooks.description: OpenCode/Codex/Cursor/Copilot hook surface coverage + per-runtime `subagent_dispatch` parity + descriptor bodies and trust hashes parity. T7 closes this row when `check validate descriptors --format json` is parity-green (24 checks: 12 codex + 12 opencode) and lifecycle adapter metadata validates.
